machine.name: Pediatric Nutrition Monitor
machine.description: Tracks child nutrition adequacy, growth trajectory, and micronutrient status for children ages 0–12 in low- and medium-income home settings. Monitors growth percentile trends, caloric adequacy, iron/vitamin D/B12 micronutrient balance, and dietary diversity — the four dimensions most predictive of developmental outcomes in under-resourced households. Output at [2003:2007] feeds the Home Caregiver Burden Monitor, as child nutritional problems are a primary driver of caregiver stress and care coordination demand.

metadata.author: Reality Engine
metadata.populationFocus: Children ages 0–12; WIC-eligible families; households with limited grocery access or food insecurity

sensorNormalization.growth_percentile_norm: 0.0=<5th percentile (failure to thrive / significant underweight), 0.5=25th–75th percentile (healthy range), 1.0=>95th percentile (overweight risk in low-income context)
sensorNormalization.caloric_adequacy_norm: 0.0=severe caloric deficit (<50% daily requirement), 0.5=adequate (90–110% daily requirement), 1.0=consistent surplus
sensorNormalization.micronutrient_score_norm: 0.0=multiple confirmed deficiencies (Fe, Vit D, B12 all low), 0.5=borderline adequate, 1.0=well-supplemented across all tracked nutrients
sensorNormalization.dietary_diversity_norm: 0.0=single food group only (calorie-dense, low-nutrient), 0.5=moderate variety (3+ food groups), 1.0=high diversity (5+ food groups consistently)

sequences.name: Growth Faltering: NORMAL+ → DECLINING → GROWTH_CONCERN → GROWTH_ALERT

metadata.description: Detects growth trajectory deviation — the most clinically significant pediatric nutrition signal. Two or more consecutive weight/height measurements crossing downward across percentile lines without illness explanation indicate nutritional inadequacy. The NORMAL+ initial state ensures baseline growth data before faltering can be flagged.

metadata.name: NORMAL
metadata.description: NORMAL+ — growth percentile in healthy range; adequate calories; acceptable micronutrient and dietary diversity scores. isInitial A*A = A+.

metadata.name: DECLINING
metadata.description: DECLINING — growth percentile crossing downward; caloric adequacy dropping; dietary diversity narrowing.

metadata.name: GROWTH_CONCERN
metadata.description: GROWTH_CONCERN — below 10th percentile with documented caloric deficit and poor dietary diversity. Fires GROWTH_ALERT requiring pediatric nutritionist or WIC referral.
GROWTH_ALERT: failure-to-thrive pattern; pediatric nutrition consult required at [2003:2007]
metadata.action: WIC referral; pediatrician growth review; food prescription program screening

sequences.name: Micronutrient Deficiency: SUFFICIENT+ → BORDERLINE → DEFICIENT → MICRONUTRIENT_CONCERN

metadata.description: Tracks micronutrient status decline — iron-deficiency anemia, vitamin D deficiency, and B12 deficiency are highly prevalent in low-income pediatric populations and cause measurable cognitive and developmental impacts. This pattern fires before overt clinical symptoms appear.

metadata.name: SUFFICIENT
metadata.description: SUFFICIENT+ — micronutrient score adequate; dietary diversity providing key vitamins and minerals. isInitial A*A = A+.

metadata.name: BORDERLINE
metadata.description: BORDERLINE — micronutrient score below adequate; caloric intake maintained but quality declining; dietary diversity narrowing.

metadata.name: DEFICIENT
metadata.description: DEFICIENT — multiple micronutrient deficiencies; low dietary diversity; caloric diet dominated by low-nutrient foods. Fires MICRONUTRIENT_CONCERN.
MICRONUTRIENT_CONCERN: iron-deficiency anemia or vitamin D/B12 deficiency risk at [2003:2007]
metadata.action: Lab screening referral; WIC food package review; supplement prescription; dietitian consult

metadata.description: Fires NUTRITION_OK when all four nutrition dimensions are above adequate thresholds. In low-income settings this represents successful outcome of WIC participation, food bank access, or community nutrition programs.

metadata.name: THRIVING
metadata.description: THRIVING+ — growth on-track, adequate calories, sufficient micronutrients, reasonable dietary diversity. Fires NUTRITION_OK each matching cycle.
metadata.scenario: Child on WIC program with consistent food access; growth tracking appropriately

metadata.description: NUTRITION_OK: pediatric nutrition adequate for developmental support at [2003:2007]
metadata.action: Caregiver burden monitor registers healthy child nutrition state

inputSequences.name: Growth Faltering During Food Assistance Gap
inputSequences.description: Child's WIC benefits lapse between recertification periods; 6-week nutrition gap causes measurable growth decline; alert prompts WIC outreach; recovery.
